problem.title: Bubble Sort
problem.description: Implement bubble sort algorithm to sort an array of integers in ascending order. Bubble sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.

example.explanation: Repeatedly swap adjacent elements that are in the wrong order until the array is sorted.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Bubble Sort Implementation [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Repeatedly sweep through the array swapping adjacent elements that are out of order, causing larger elements to "bubble" toward the end. An early-exit flag (`swapped`) allows the algorithm to terminate in O(n) when the array is already sorted, which is the key optimization over a naive implementation.

complexity.time: O(n²) worst and average case, O(n) best case
